title: Mass. man charged with threatening FBI director’s girlfriend
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden Welch Ruml, 26, from Cambridge, charged with threatening FBI Director Kash Patel's girlfriend via email.
The threatening email to Alexis Wilkins was sent after Ruml was upset by a news article about her.
Ruml pleaded not guilty; faces up to five years in prison if convicted, according to U.S. Attorney's Office.

title: Board a lobster boat and dine on your catch while staying at this Boston hotel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Lenox in Boston offers a lobster-catching experience with a private tour on a working Boston Harbor lobster boat.
Guests enjoy a five-course tasting menu with their catch, prepared by The Lenox's Executive Chef Daniel Kenney.
The Boston Harbor Lobster Experience Package includes transportation, accommodations, and costs $1,650 for two or $1,950 for four people.

title: Red Line car shells cleared from customs, reviving replacement project
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: U.S. customs released detained Red Line car shells, allowing CRRC to resume manufacturing in Springfield.
The release followed negotiations by Rep. Richard Neal and Gov. Maura Healey to clear customs issues.
142 furloughed employees to return as CRRC ramps up operations to complete 190 remaining Red Line cars.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to fight addiction stigma
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kevin Hayden of North Easton will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Learn to Cope, a nonprofit aiding families impacted by addiction.
Hayden aims to raise awareness and challenge addiction stigma by running in memory of friends and loved ones lost to addiction.
Learn to Cope provides education, peer-led support, and seeks to reduce stigma around addiction as a public health issue.

title: Social media celeb Alix Earle returned to Harvard this week
author: John Waller
bullets: Alix Earle visited Harvard University to speak with Business School students and initiate a case study.
Earle spoke in Professor Reza Satchu’s entrepreneurship class, joining previous guest Reese Witherspoon.
Earle previously visited Harvard, celebrating with a reference to 'Legally Blonde's' Elle Woods on social media.

title: ‘There’s nothing compared to that feeling’: Red Sox players discussed return from World Baseball Classic
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Red Sox players, including Wilyer Abreu and Willson Contreras, returned to spring training after participating in the World Baseball Classic.
Venezuela won its first World Baseball Classic title, defeating Japan and the USA with contributions from Red Sox pitcher Ranger Suarez.
Opening Day for the Red Sox is March 26 against the Cincinnati Reds, following their return from the international tournament.

title: Marlon Wayans stopped by this South Boston restaurant for lunch
author: John Waller
bullets: Marlon Wayans visited Hunter's Kitchen & Bar in South Boston for lunch while on his 'Wild Child Tour.'
Wayans appreciated the southern cuisine, sharing an Instagram story praising chef Lambert 'Lambo' Givens's dishes.
After visiting Boston, Wayans's tour continues with a performance at the Zeiterion Theatre in New Bedford.

title: Nine Winters Bakery is closing in Cambridge: ‘I’m choosing my family’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Nine Winters Bakery in Cambridge will close on March 29 after less than a year of operation.
Owner Marissa Ferola is closing the Korean-American bakery to focus on her family and personal life.
The bakery, initially a pop-up, offered unique treats like Gochujang sticky buns and kimchi cheese danishes.

title: Stephen Curry swung by this North End restaurant while in town for Celtics-Warriors
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Curry visited Nick Varano's Strega in Boston's North End while in town for a Celtics-Warriors game.
Curry, currently sidelined with a knee injury, attended the Celtics game where Boston won 120-99.
Strega's Instagram shared photos and videos of Curry, noting his friendly association with the restaurant.

title: It’s finally payday for former Boston Sports Clubs members
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office to distribute $4 million to 47,000 former Boston Sports Clubs members.
Court ruled CEO Patrick Walsh unlawfully charged members during pandemic shutdowns and violated cancellation rights.
Refunds of approximately $85 per member; claim instructions sent via email to affected consumers.
